sumzero_ideas.notes: Local SumZero Ideas archive index; raw documents stay out of git.

pdf_store.notes: Google Drive canonical PDF store; orphan count is unmanaged legacy PDFs retained under canonical folders.

terminalvalue_candidates.notes: Provider candidates for fundamentals, filings, transcripts, ownership, news, macro and valuation feeds.

research_archive.notes: Full raw insight records are kept out of the front dashboard payload.

selected_tools.name: Better EDGAR
selected_tools.dashboard_role: SEC filing retrieval and cleaner document discovery

terminalvalue.title: Better EDGAR
terminalvalue.description: The EDGAR you wish existed — cross-issuer search, a live filings feed, enriched 8-K/6-K exhibits, Form 4 + 13F, and email/RSS/JSON/MCP/API delivery.
terminalvalue.key_feature: Filings news feed + cross-issuer search (no ticker required)
terminalvalue.best_for: monitoring sec filing activity and market developments across multiple issuers or sectors simultaneously
terminalvalue.overview: sec filing dashboard and search tool that surfaces recent 8-k/6-k filings, insider form 4 trades, and 13f holdings across all issuers without requiring a ticker first. supports cross-issuer filtering by market cap, sector, form type, 8-k item, and exhibit label; delivers results via web, email digest, rss, json api, or mcp integration to chatgpt/claude.

selected_tools.name: Daloopa
selected_tools.dashboard_role: normalized KPIs and model-ready company data

terminalvalue.title: Daloopa
terminalvalue.description: AI-extracted, audit-ready financial data for 5,500+ global tickers — every number hyperlinked to its primary source for one-click verification.
terminalvalue.key_feature: >99% accurate, source-linked financials with Excel add-in
terminalvalue.best_for: analysts at hedge funds, mutual funds, and investment banks building and updating financial models — also supports builders creating financial ai agents.
terminalvalue.overview: ai-extracted fundamental data for 5,500+ global public companies, delivered via excel add-in, api, mcp server, and data sheets. every data point hyperlinked to its original source filing (sec, press releases, investor presentations, transcripts) for one-click verification. covers 14 years of history with 4-10x more kpis per ticker than other providers, updated during earnings with >99% accuracy.

terminalvalue.title: EDGAR Analyst
terminalvalue.description: Pulls SEC filings from EDGAR, extracts structured data, redlines disclosure changes across periods, and generates cited analysis within 60 seconds of filing publication
terminalvalue.key_feature: word-level redlines across filing periods with materiality scoring
terminalvalue.overview: pulls sec filings from edgar, extracts structured line items from tables/segments/footnotes, redlines word-level changes across filing periods with materiality scoring, and generates cited analysis within 60 seconds of publication. runs monitors on any ticker that trigger custom analysis on new filings and deliver results via email/slack/webhook before press releases land.
terminalvalue.best_for: hedge fund analysts, sell-side researchers, family office portfolio managers, and private equity teams reading sec filings under time pressure.
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: Free reader tier visible, paid plans with 7-day trial mentioned but no numeric pricing shown on any page; pricing inferred as FREE-$$ based on freemium SaaS pattern; SOC2 Type II in progress signals p

selected_tools.name: Earnings API
selected_tools.dashboard_role: earnings calendar and reported earnings coverage

terminalvalue.title: Earnings API
terminalvalue.description: Provides REST API access to historical and upcoming earnings calendar data for NYSE and NASDAQ stocks with date-based queries, post-earnings reactions, and structured JSON responses for automated workflows.
terminalvalue.key_feature: date-based earnings calendar with beat/miss data plus post-report price/volume
terminalvalue.overview: rest api returning historical and upcoming earnings calendar data for nyse and nasdaq stocks. date-based queries for pre/after-market earnings schedules, estimate vs. actual eps and revenue, plus optional post-earnings price/volume reactions. structured json for backfills, daily syncs, and automation workflows.
terminalvalue.best_for: fintech products building earnings alerts, newsletters, quant dashboards, or research platforms that need calendar imports and scheduled event syncs.
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: Free tier 100/day, 1k/mo. Paid tiers not shown but implied by 'Paid access' mention. Company (ToS, privacy, dashboard infra). Earnings calendar + reactions = core product.

selected_tools.name: FRED MCP
selected_tools.dashboard_role: macro series for rates, spreads, inflation, liquidity and commodities

terminalvalue.title: Financial Datasets
terminalvalue.description: Stock-market data API built for AI agents — financials, SEC filings, KPIs, insider trades, and analyst estimates across 17,000+ tickers and 30+ years of history.
terminalvalue.key_feature: API designed natively for LLM agents (not human dashboards)
terminalvalue.best_for: ai financial agents, quantitative research platforms, portfolio management systems, and event-driven trading infrastructure
terminalvalue.overview: stock-market data api serving real-time and historical financials, sec filings, kpis, insider trades, institutional holdings, and price data across 27,000+ us tickers with 30+ years of history. designed for programmatic consumption by ai agents and llm pipelines — structured json responses, section-level filing access, data available within seconds of publication.

selected_tools.name: Fiscal AI
selected_tools.dashboard_role: fundamental research and company model enrichment

terminalvalue.title: Fiscal AI
terminalvalue.description: Conversational AI research over institutional-grade financials for 100k+ global companies, powered by S&P Market Intelligence.
terminalvalue.key_feature: Natural-language queries across S&P-grade fundamentals + segment KPIs
terminalvalue.best_for: investors needing natural-language access to s&p-grade company financials and segment data
terminalvalue.overview: conversational ai interface for querying institutional-grade financial data on 100k+ global companies. uses natural language to search s&p market intelligence fundamentals and segment-level kpis.

selected_tools.name: Quartr
selected_tools.dashboard_role: earnings call transcripts, investor events and presentation coverage

terminalvalue.title: Quartr
terminalvalue.description: Live earnings calls, transcripts, slides, filings, and analyst estimates for 14k+ public companies — mobile, web, and API.
terminalvalue.key_feature: Live earnings audio + real-time transcripts for 14k+ companies
terminalvalue.best_for: finance professionals, financial institutions, research platforms, and ai companies needing structured investor relations data
terminalvalue.overview: ai infrastructure for company research providing live earnings calls, transcripts, filings, and slides from 14k+ public companies structured for ai. two offerings: quartr pro (ai research platform for finance professionals) and quartr api (ir data layer for builders), delivering real-time first-party data across 65+ markets.

selected_tools.name: ROIC AI
selected_tools.dashboard_role: quality, returns on capital and valuation context

terminalvalue.title: ROIC AI
terminalvalue.description: Financial analysis platform with 30+ years of statements, transcripts, screener, and a powerful API for 60k+ tickers.
terminalvalue.key_feature: 30-yr financials + ROIC-focused metrics with API & Sheets add-in
terminalvalue.best_for: investors who want comprehensive historical financials and ROIC-focused fundamental analysis in one place, and developers building applications with a financial data API.
terminalvalue.overview: financial data platform and API serving 30+ years of statements, pre-calculated fundamental ratios (ROIC, ROE, debt-to-EBITDA), earnings call transcripts, and stock prices for 60,000+ public companies across 70+ global exchanges. delivers data as JSON or TSV for spreadsheets; includes web interface for quick company lookup and AI assistant for queries.

selected_tools.name: Stock Titan
selected_tools.dashboard_role: company news and press release monitoring

terminalvalue.title: Stock Titan
terminalvalue.description: Provides real-time stock news feed with AI sentiment and impact scoring, momentum alerts, SEC filings monitoring, and keyword-based filtering for individual equities.
terminalvalue.key_feature: real-time AI sentiment & impact scoring (1-5 scale) on every news article
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: pricing from homepage/pricing: Free, $19.99/mo Silver, $59.99/mo Gold, $89.99/mo Platinum. Highest visible = $89.99/mo = $$. Range: FREE-$$. Asset_class: focuses on individual stock tickers (NYSE/NASD
terminalvalue.best_for: traders who need to read company press releases and filings the moment they're filed, before price moves fully reflect the news
terminalvalue.overview: stock titan delivers a real-time news feed filtered to individual equities, with AI (rhea) scoring each article for sentiment and impact on a 1-5 scale. includes momentum scanner (argus) that flags stocks moving on breaking news, SEC filings monitoring with AI summaries, and keyword/float/market-cap/price filters to build custom feeds.

selected_tools.name: Unusual Whales
selected_tools.dashboard_role: options flow, unusual volume and insider/trading context

terminalvalue.title: Unusual Whales
terminalvalue.description: Tracks real-time options flow, dark pool activity, congressional trading, Greek exposure, and unusual stock market data via web platform and API with 100+ endpoints.
terminalvalue.key_feature: real-time options flow tracking with dark pool and congressional trading data
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: pricing page meta exists but no visible numeric tiers extracted; multiple product tiers mentioned (free tier, API at $250/mo historical option trades, Discord bot) but exact monthly pricing not visibl
terminalvalue.best_for: retail traders spotting institutional moves and analyzing options flow
terminalvalue.overview: unusual whales tracks real-time options flow, dark pool activity, congressional trading, greek exposure, and unusual stock market data. delivers via web platform, mobile app, api with 100+ endpoints, websocket/kafka streaming, and mcp server integration. covers 8,000+ tickers with real-time and historical data.

terminalvalue.title: ValueInvesting.io
terminalvalue.description: Generates automated discounted cash flow valuations and intrinsic value estimates for over 45,000 stocks across 60 global exchanges using public data and consensus forecasts.
terminalvalue.key_feature: pre-calculated DCF models for all global stocks with modifiable assumptions
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: pricing from $14.99/mo Regional ($$) and $42.99/mo Global ($$$) annual plans; no enterprise tier pricing visible; company entity implied by team/contact structure
terminalvalue.best_for: value investors seeking pre-calculated intrinsic value models to screen undervalued stocks globally without building dcfs manually
terminalvalue.overview: automated dcf valuations for 45,000+ stocks across 60 global exchanges using public data, damodaran estimates, and consensus forecasts. includes pre-built models with modifiable assumptions, stock screener, ai-powered filings search, and unlimited data export. offers regional and global access tiers.

terminalvalue.title: WhaleWisdom
terminalvalue.description: Tracks institutional investor 13F filings, insider transactions, 13D/13G disclosures, and mutual fund holdings with backtesting and portfolio replication tools
terminalvalue.key_feature: 20+ years of 13F data with fund-level backtesting and consensus holdings analysis
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: Pricing from /pricing: Free tier (2 years data) + Standard $90/quarter + Pro $150/quarter. Pro = $$. Enterprise = contact sales. API access on Standard+. Upper bound = $150/qtr = $50/mo = $$.
terminalvalue.best_for: analyzing and replicating institutional investor portfolios using quarterly 13F and Schedule 13D/G data.
terminalvalue.overview: tracks 13F filings, 13D/13G disclosures, insider transactions, and mutual fund holdings for institutional investors and hedge funds. offers 20+ years of historical data, backtesting tools to evaluate fund performance, and portfolio replication. provides web interface, excel add-in, screeners, and a developer API with digital signature authentication.

META.title: 1 Massively Undervalued Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock That Could Be Worth More Than SpaceX Within 12 Months
META.claim: The article argues that SpaceX's IPO valuation of $2.2 trillion is unsustainable due to extremely high multiples (100x+ sales), aggressive growth projections, and upcoming share lockup expirations that will pressure the stock. In contrast, Meta Platforms is positioned to generate significant financial gains from AI investments at a more attractive valuation of 17x forward earnings, with strong ad revenue growth (33% in Q1 2026) driven by AI improvements in content curation and targeting.

META.title: Prediction: 3 Trillion-Dollar Stocks Set to Leapfrog SpaceX by the End of 2026
META.claim: SpaceX's $75 billion IPO on June 12, 2026 briefly valued the company at nearly $3 trillion, but the article argues this valuation is unsustainable. Historical data shows mega-IPOs average a 55% year-one drawdown. The article predicts three established trillion-dollar companies—Meta Platforms, Berkshire Hathaway, and Broadcom—will surpass SpaceX's market cap by end of 2026 due to SpaceX's insider lockup schedule, lack of recurring profits, and structural headwinds.

META.title: Beyond the Trillion-Dollar Club: 3 High-Growth Tech Stocks to Buy Right Now
META.claim: The article highlights three high-growth tech stocks outside the trillion-dollar club with significant upside potential: Sandisk (NAND memory for data centers), Nebius (AI data center infrastructure), and CoreWeave (AI computing resources). All three companies are experiencing rapid revenue growth driven by massive demand for AI and data center infrastructure, with projected growth rates significantly exceeding market averages.

META.title: Nebius Is Up 210% This Year. Here's Why There's Still Room for More Upside in 2026.
META.claim: Nebius, a Nvidia-backed neocloud AI computing platform company, has surged 210% year-to-date and is expected to continue growing significantly. With Q1 revenue up 684% year-over-year and Wall Street projecting 550% growth for 2026 and 225% for 2027, the company's potential may not be fully priced in yet. Major clients including Microsoft and Meta are driving this explosive growth.

GOOGL.title: SpaceX Is Quietly Becoming One of the Most Important Data Center Companies in AI. Here's What That Means for Investors
GOOGL.claim: SpaceX has announced three major AI compute deals with Anthropic, Alphabet/Google, and Reflection AI, generating approximately $27.5 billion in annual recurring revenue. The company is monetizing excess GPU capacity from its xAI platform, transforming its business model to include a significant high-margin data center revenue stream that could rival its traditional rocket launch business.

GOOGL.title: Move Over, Coca-Cola: Warren Buffett's Successor, Greg Abel, Has a New No. 3 Holding at Berkshire Hathaway
GOOGL.claim: Greg Abel, Warren Buffett's successor as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, has significantly increased the company's stake in Alphabet through a $10 billion private placement, elevating it to the No. 3 position in Berkshire's portfolio ahead of long-held Coca-Cola. The move signals Abel's confidence in Alphabet's AI leadership and cloud growth potential, marking a shift toward tech investments that Buffett historically avoided.

GOOGL.title: Does a Lower Stock Price and New Contracts Make SpaceX Stock a Buy?
GOOGL.claim: SpaceX stock has fallen 23% from its peak and now trades near its IPO price. The company has secured three major AI infrastructure contracts worth $76.3 billion combined, which would boost 2026 revenue to $46.5 billion. However, at 44x forward sales, SpaceX remains expensive compared to peers like Palantir (36x sales). While the company has significant growth potential in a $28.5 trillion addressable market, investors should approach cautiously with small positions in a diversified portfolio.

GOOGL.title: SpaceX Won't Use Traditional Wire Services to Share Financial Results. What That Means for Investors.
GOOGL.claim: SpaceX announced it will share quarterly and annual financial results through its investor relations website and X account instead of traditional wire services like Business Wire or PR Newswire. While the company remains compliant with SEC requirements, this strategy places greater responsibility on investors to actively monitor SpaceX's disclosures. The move comes as SpaceX operates an increasingly complex business spanning satellite internet (Starlink), AI infrastructure, and space launch services.

GOOGL.title: Alphabet’s $225 Billion Wipeout Looks Like a Narrative Break
GOOGL.claim: Alphabet lost $225 billion in market value after two senior AI researchers (Nobel laureate John Jumper and VP Noam Shazeer) departed for competitors Anthropic and OpenAI. The selloff reflects market concerns about Google losing the AI talent war, the commoditization of AI models, massive capex spending ($180-190B annually) with collapsing free cash flow, and regulatory headwinds. While fundamentals remain strong, the narrative around Google's AI competitive position has broken.

GOOGL.title: The Chip Sell-Off Is Not Just About AI: There’s a Bigger Problem Under the Hood
GOOGL.claim: Tuesday's semiconductor sell-off extends beyond AI concerns to reveal structural market issues. Leveraged ETFs like SOXL use daily rebalancing that amplifies downturns, forcing selling that exacerbates price declines. South Korea's regulatory warning about overheated leveraged chip ETFs triggered a 10% Kospi drop, affecting global memory chip stocks. The sector faces a critical test with Micron's earnings on June 24, where forward guidance on HBM demand and data center orders will determine if the AI spending cycle remains intact.

GOOGL.title: Got $1,000? Why Broadcom's Guidance Created the Ultimate Artificial Intelligence (AI) Buying Opportunity.
GOOGL.claim: Broadcom experienced a sharp sell-off after Q2 earnings despite not significantly raising 2027 guidance, which the author views as an irrational market reaction. The company's custom AI chips (ASICs) for major clients like Alphabet, Meta, OpenAI, and Anthropic are positioned to drive AI semiconductor revenue to over $100 billion by 2027. Despite a 35x forward earnings valuation, the author considers it a buying opportunity given the multi-year AI growth trajectory and potential 21x multiple based on 2027 earnings.

GOOGL.title: CureWise Founder and CEO Steve Brown to Speak at Aspen Ideas: Health 2026 and Launch AI-Powered Precision Oncology Platform
GOOGL.claim: CureWise, an AI-powered precision oncology platform, officially launched at Aspen Ideas: Health 2026. Founded by Steve Brown following his rare cancer diagnosis, the platform helps cancer patients understand their disease, explore personalized treatment options, and advocate for targeted therapies by securely integrating medical records and leveraging multiple AI models to identify relevant clinical trials.

GOOGL.title: SpaceX and Nvidia Each Forecast $1 Trillion in Revenue. Which Stock is the Better Buy?
GOOGL.claim: Both SpaceX and Nvidia have ambitious $1 trillion revenue forecasts, but Nvidia presents a clearer path to achieving this goal with strong AI chip demand and a reasonable valuation at 23x forward earnings. SpaceX, despite recent IPO success and growth in Starlink, faces heavy capital expenditures and a more uncertain timeline to reach $1 trillion by 2030, making Nvidia the better buy currently.

GOOGL.title: Don't Forget About Western Digital Amid Sandisk Euphoria
GOOGL.claim: While Sandisk has dominated headlines with an 820% year-to-date gain, Western Digital deserves attention as another memory stock benefiting from AI infrastructure buildout. Western Digital's hard disk drives are critical for AI chips to store and process vast amounts of data. The company posted 45% year-over-year revenue growth and 11% sequential growth in Q3 fiscal 2026, with guidance for 9.4% sequential growth in Q4. With 809 planned data centers and AI spending expected to continue for years, Western Digital is well-positioned as a leading HDD provider.

GOOGL.title: Anthropic Filed a Confidential S-1 at a $965 Billion Valuation. Here's How to Buy in Right Now.
GOOGL.claim: Anthropic, valued at $965 billion, filed confidentially with the SEC to go public on June 1. Most investors cannot buy shares directly before the IPO, but the KraneShares Artificial Intelligence and Technology ETF (AGIX) already holds approximately 17,800 shares of Anthropic (1.4% of holdings). The ETF provides broad exposure to AI companies across hardware, infrastructure, and applications sectors, with top public holdings including Nvidia, Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft, and Apple. The ETF has gained over 27% in 2026 but carries risks including potential volatility around the IPO and concentration in AI stocks.

GOOGL.title: Why This $26.5 Trillion Projection Is Critical to Understand Before Buying SpaceX Stock
GOOGL.claim: SpaceX's S-1 filing projects a $28.5 trillion total addressable market, with $26.5 trillion tied to AI opportunities including space-based data centers. Goldman Sachs forecasts SpaceX's AI division revenue could surge from $3.2 billion in 2025 to $322 billion by 2030. However, the company is in early stages of AI development with significant execution risks, spending $6.4 billion in 2025 operating losses and $12.7 billion in AI capital expenditures.

GOOGL.title: What Does Berkshire Hathaway See in This Housing Stock?
GOOGL.claim: Berkshire Hathaway's new CEO Greg Abel announced a $8.5 billion acquisition of homebuilder Taylor Morrison despite current weakness in the housing market. Abel is following Warren Buffett's contrarian investment philosophy by buying when others are fearful, betting on a cyclical recovery while the U.S. faces a shortage of 10 million homes.

GOOGL.title: 3 Elite Trillion-Dollar Giants Worth Loading Up On Right Now
GOOGL.claim: The article highlights three trillion-dollar companies—Walmart, Costco, and Berkshire Hathaway—as compelling investment opportunities. Walmart leverages AI and same-day delivery reaching 95% of U.S. households to drive growth. Costco maintains strong member loyalty with rising transaction sizes and membership fees. Berkshire Hathaway, under new CEO Greg Abel, is expanding into homebuilding and increasing its Alphabet stake, positioning itself as an underappreciated consumer story.

GOOGL.title: Goldman Sachs Says SpaceX Could Hit $474 Billion in Revenue by 2030. Here Are the 3 AI Stocks That Benefit Most.
GOOGL.claim: Goldman Sachs forecasts SpaceX will generate $474 billion in total revenue by 2030, with $322 billion from its AI division. Three companies stand to benefit significantly: Nvidia will supply advanced chips for SpaceX's data centers, Alphabet holds a 5-6% stake and rents computing capacity, and Tesla will benefit from a shared semiconductor manufacturing facility (Terafab) with SpaceX.

NVDA.title: System on Module Market Size to Hit USD 6.81 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
NVDA.claim: The global System on Module (SOM) market, valued at USD 3.00 billion in 2025, is expected to grow to USD 6.81 billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 8.57%. Growth is driven by expanding IoT adoption, edge AI technologies, and industrial automation. ARM Architecture dominates with 72.35% market share, while COM-HPC is the fastest-growing standard. North America leads regionally, with Asia-Pacific showing the fastest growth.

NVDA.title: Micron’s Blowout Is About to Test the Summer Fade the Rally Crowd
NVDA.claim: Micron delivered a blowout earnings report with revenue of $41.46B (vs. $36B expected) and guided Q4 revenue to ~$50B (vs. $43B Street estimate). The company signed 16 multi-year Strategic Customer Agreements worth ~$100B in contracted revenue, signaling a structural shift in the memory industry from cyclical boom-bust patterns to more stable, contracted cash flows. This demonstrates that AI demand continues to outrun memory supply, giving producers strong pricing power. However, investors should note that betting against Micron now requires believing that shortages, hyperscaler spending, and customer urgency all cool simultaneously.

NVDA.title: Could SpaceX Stock Make You a Millionaire? This Is the Simple Answer
NVDA.claim: SpaceX's $2 trillion valuation following its record $75 billion IPO limits its potential for explosive returns. While the company has ambitious plans for interplanetary travel and a $28.5 trillion addressable market, achieving 10x returns would require it to become the world's most valuable company by a significant margin—a difficult feat given the law of large numbers. Most wealth creation from SpaceX occurred in private markets before the IPO.

NVDA.title: Bitget 推出 Stock+，讓加密原生投資者輕鬆買賣真實美股
NVDA.claim: Bitget has officially launched Stock+, a new feature within its Stocks 2.0 ecosystem that allows users to purchase real US stocks directly using USDC and other digital assets. The platform offers actual stock ownership through regulated brokers, dividend rights, and stock split adjustments, with trading synchronized to US market hours. This represents Bitget's evolution toward a universal exchange combining cryptocurrency and traditional financial markets in a single account.

NVDA.title: Better Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock to Buy: SpaceX vs. Nvidia
NVDA.claim: The article compares SpaceX and Nvidia as AI investment options. While SpaceX recently acquired xAI and owns the X social media platform, its AI division generated only $3.2 billion in revenue with 22% growth. Nvidia, by contrast, is growing at 85% year-over-year with 96% projected growth in the current quarter, generating over $250 billion in annual revenue. Despite SpaceX's $2 trillion market cap being 40% of Nvidia's $5 trillion valuation, SpaceX's 2025 revenue was less than $20 billion with $6.6 billion in adjusted EBITDA—far below expectations for its valuation. The author concludes Nvidia is the better AI stock choice due to more reasonable valuation and stronger AI business fundamentals.

NVDA.title: Super Micro Computer Is Trading at Its Best Valuation in 18 Months. Should You Buy the Dip?
NVDA.claim: Super Micro Computer's stock has recovered from fraud allegations but faces significant headwinds. Despite 250% revenue growth since early 2024, profits only grew 70% due to margin pressure from commoditized products. A $7 billion equity offering will dilute shareholders. Trading at under 10x forward earnings, the stock appears cheap but the author recommends Nvidia instead due to Supermicro's uncertain future and lack of pricing power.

NVDA.title: SpaceX Valuation Loses Altitude as AI Risks Mount
NVDA.claim: SpaceX's stock has fallen 25% from its $225 IPO peak to $165, as investors reassess the company's strategic pivot toward AI data centers and software acquisitions. The $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere, combined with a $6.3 billion computing power agreement with Reflection AI, is straining cash flow and diluting shareholder value. Additionally, Starlink's average revenue per user has declined from $99 to $66 monthly, while the company faces potential merger rumors with Tesla. Rising interest rates and a microscopic 5% public float are amplifying the sell-off.

NVDA.title: This Nvidia-Backed Company Just Entered the $154 Billion AI Photonics Market. Does This Make the $13 Stock a No-Brainer Buy?
NVDA.claim: Nokia is expanding its role in AI infrastructure through silicon photonics and optical networking solutions. The company announced expansion of photonic chip operations in Pennsylvania and reported strong Q1 2026 results with 49% YoY growth in its AI & Cloud segment and $1.1 billion in new AI infrastructure orders. Goldman Sachs projects the optical networking market tied to AI will grow from $15 billion in 2026 to $154 billion by 2028, with co-packaged optics representing a $91 billion opportunity. Recent insider buying by Nokia executives, combined with Nvidia's $1 billion strategic investment, suggests the stock has further upside potential.

The article argues that unlike typical cyclical memory chip markets, the current AI infrastructure buildout could represent an unusually long demand cycle, with data center capex expected to reach $1 trillion in 2027 and $3-4 trillion by 2030.

NVDA.title: This Healthcare Stock Trades at a Richer Valuation Than Nvidia. Could It Also Deliver Bigger Long-Term Returns?
NVDA.claim: While Nvidia leads the AI revolution with a 31x P/E ratio, Intuitive Surgical trades at 50x P/E but appears cheaper relative to its historical 70x average. The surgical robotics leader generates recurring revenue from parts and services (75% of revenue) supporting its da Vinci robots, offering more stable long-term growth than Nvidia's competitive chip market. With expanding FDA approvals for new applications, Intuitive Surgical presents a compelling growth opportunity for aggressive investors.

NVDA.title: OpenAI's Massive Losses Strengthen the Bull Case for These 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks
NVDA.claim: OpenAI's leaked financials reveal a $20.92 billion operating loss in 2025 despite $13.07 billion in revenue, driven by massive spending on AI infrastructure and hardware. This heavy spending benefits Nvidia, which supplies critical GPU chips for AI training, and Microsoft, which provides cloud infrastructure through Azure. Both companies are well-positioned to capitalize on continued AI industry investment.

NVDA.title: Super Micro Jumped More Than 10%. Is the AI Server Maker Finally Turning a Corner?
NVDA.claim: Super Micro Computer's stock surged over 10% on Thursday despite no major company news. The AI server maker's fiscal Q3 revenue more than doubled to $10.2 billion, and gross margins recovered to 9.9% from 6.3% in the prior quarter. However, concerns remain regarding the company's rising debt load ($8.8 billion), ongoing export-control investigation, and whether it can convert $39 billion in new orders into profitable revenue.

NVDA.title: After Being Called the Next Trillion-Dollar AI Stock and Joining the S&P 500, Marvell's CFO Just Filed to Dump $65 Million of Stock. Here's Why.
NVDA.claim: Marvell Technology's CFO Willem Meintjes filed to sell approximately 211,000 shares worth $65 million following Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's endorsement calling Marvell the next trillion-dollar semiconductor stock and the company's S&P 500 inclusion announcement. However, the sale is attributed to Meintjes' planned departure after 10 years in the role, with an experienced successor already in place, suggesting the transaction reflects normal personal financial planning rather than underlying company concerns.

NVDA.title: Marvell Technology Skyrocketed on a Stunning Nvidia Endorsement: Could It Be the Next Trillion-Dollar AI Stock?
NVDA.claim: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang endorsed Marvell Technology as a potential trillion-dollar company, causing its stock to surge over 40% since early June. However, the article argues that despite strong growth prospects in AI networking and custom chips, Marvell's valuation at 76x forward earnings is too expensive compared to Nvidia at 24x forward earnings, making it a less attractive investment despite its industry tailwinds.

AMZN.title: Cloud Discovery Market Size to Surpass $9.77 Billion by 2035 as Multi-Cloud Adoption and Shadow IT Challenges Drive Enterprise Demand | Report by SNS Insider
AMZN.claim: The global cloud discovery market is projected to grow from $1.92 billion in 2025 to $9.77 billion by 2035 at a CAGR of 17.24%. Growth is driven by multi-cloud complexity, shadow IT risks, and regulatory requirements. North America leads with 43% market share, while Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing region. Public cloud and security/compliance applications dominate current segments.

AMZN.title: Meet the Growth Stock That's Cheaper Than 6 "Magnificent Seven" Stocks. Here's Why It's a No-Brainer Buy Before the End of June.
AMZN.claim: Netflix stock has fallen 22.3% year-to-date and now trades at a cheaper valuation than six of the Magnificent Seven stocks. Despite concerns about acquisition attempts and price hikes, the article argues Netflix remains an excellent buy due to its strong content pipeline, international revenue diversification, and low forward P/E ratio of 20.2x compared to the S&P 500's 22.4x. The company is positioned as a non-AI growth stock offering portfolio diversification.

AMZN.title: Stock Market Today, June 24: Micron Surges After Hours as Nasdaq Stems Losses
AMZN.claim: U.S. stock indexes showed mixed performance on June 24, 2026, with the Dow gaining 0.35% while the Nasdaq fell 0.43% amid semiconductor volatility. Micron Technology surged over 13% in after-hours trading following strong earnings and guidance. Other notable moves included Wendy's jumping 26%, Hertz tumbling 40% after a stock offering announcement, and Cerebras Systems crashing on its first earnings report as a public company. JPMorgan raised its year-end S&P 500 target to 7,800.

AMZN.title: Better Buy: SpaceX or the "Magnificent Seven"?
AMZN.claim: SpaceX's recent IPO has brought it to trillion-dollar valuation comparable to tech giants, but the article argues it remains overvalued compared to the Magnificent Seven. While SpaceX generated $18.7B in revenue in 2025 with a negative 26% profit margin, achieving its 45% target margin would still leave it far behind established tech leaders in absolute profits. The analysis suggests SpaceX would need seven years of 40% annual growth to match Amazon's profit levels, meaning significant future growth is already priced into the stock. The author recommends the Magnificent Seven companies as better investments with less speculative valuation.

AMZN.title: LoRaWAN Industry Analysis and Revenue Forecast 2026-2032 - Industrial IoT Use Cases in Predictive Maintenance Accelerate Network Deployment
AMZN.claim: The global LoRaWAN market is projected to grow from US$3.6 billion in 2025 to US$44.2 billion by 2032, with a CAGR of 43.1%. Growth is driven by increasing IoT adoption, smart city initiatives, industrial predictive maintenance use cases, and expanding LPWAN demand across utilities, agriculture, and manufacturing sectors.

AMZN.title: Bill Ackman Says These 3 Stocks Could Be Like Buying Berkshire Hathaway in 2000
AMZN.claim: Bill Ackman compares Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft to Berkshire Hathaway in 2000, arguing these tech giants are undervalued as investors chase AI and semiconductor stocks. Despite concerns about their capital spending, Ackman believes their strong revenue growth and competitive advantages position them for significant outperformance over the next decade.

AMZN.title: Alphabet's Google Cloud Backlog Just Hit a Record $462 Billion. Time to Buy the Stock?
AMZN.claim: Alphabet's Google Cloud division has reached a record $462 billion backlog with 63% year-over-year revenue growth, outpacing competitors AWS (28%) and Azure (40%). The company is investing heavily in data center infrastructure, with 2026 capex guidance of $180-190 billion and plans for even higher spending in 2027. This positions Alphabet for continued growth and a transformation from an advertising-focused to a more balanced cloud-driven business.

AMZN.title: Google Tests Major Support as AI Spending Fears Return
AMZN.claim: Alphabet (Google) shares fell 5% as investors question whether massive AI infrastructure spending will generate sufficient returns. While Google's fundamentals remain strong with 22% revenue growth and $460B+ cloud backlog, the market demands proof that extraordinary AI investments will create proportional returns. The stock has reached a technical support zone at $337.47-$349.00, with potential for a relief rally if it reclaims $349-$352, though broader macro headwinds from rising Treasury yields and Fed tightening concerns persist.

AMZN.title: After Missing Out on Roku, Netflix Claims It Won't Buy Lionsgate. Here's Why the Market Hates That Answer.
AMZN.claim: Netflix's stock has fallen 17.5% year-to-date after losing bidding wars for Warner Bros. Discovery and Roku, and denying interest in acquiring Lionsgate. However, the article argues the market is overreacting, as Netflix's business model has shifted to prioritizing original content rather than legacy libraries, with strong financial performance including 47% revenue growth and 215% net income growth over three years.

AMZN.title: Set It and Forget It: 2 Consumer Stocks to Hold for the Next 20 Years
AMZN.claim: The article recommends Amazon and Costco as long-term 'set and forget' stocks suitable for 20-year holding periods. Amazon benefits from its dominance in e-commerce and cloud computing (AWS), which is experiencing explosive growth from the AI boom. Costco's membership-based model with high renewal rates (92% in U.S./Canada) and low-margin warehouse sales offset by high-margin membership fees provide stability across market conditions.

AMZN.title: Better Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock to Buy: Broadcom vs. Marvell Technology
AMZN.claim: Broadcom and Marvell Technology both help AI hyperscalers design custom AI chips (ASICs). While both companies have strong growth prospects, Broadcom is identified as the superior investment due to its stronger client base (Alphabet, Meta, OpenAI, Anthropic), better flagship custom AI chip (Google's TPU), higher projected revenue growth (66% and 62% for FY2026-2027 vs. Marvell's 41-45%), and more attractive valuation at 34x forward earnings compared to Marvell's higher multiple.

AMZN.title: 5 Dividend Stocks to Buy and Hold Forever
AMZN.claim: The article highlights five blue-chip dividend stocks suitable for long-term buy-and-hold investing: AbbVie, McDonald's, Johnson & Johnson, Walmart, and Becton Dickinson. These companies have demonstrated decades of consecutive dividend increases and strong financial fundamentals, with dividends well-covered by earnings and solid growth prospects ahead.

AMZN.title: CoreWeave is Joining the Nasdaq-100. Is the Stock a Buy?
AMZN.claim: CoreWeave, an AI infrastructure company providing GPU compute access, is joining the Nasdaq-100 index on June 22, 2026. The stock has surged 194% since its IPO over a year ago, driven by triple-digit revenue growth and a $100 billion contracted order backlog. While the Nasdaq-100 inclusion may provide short-term momentum, the article suggests buying CoreWeave for its long-term growth potential, strong Nvidia partnership, and increasing customer diversification—though it carries risks due to high leverage and debt reliance.

AMZN.title: In 10 Years, Will You Wish You'd Bought This Industrial Stock Right Now?
AMZN.claim: United Parcel Service (UPS) is undergoing a major corporate overhaul to become leaner and more profitable, including reducing low-margin customers like Amazon. Despite near-term earnings challenges from infrastructure investments and business restructuring, the company shows progress with rising revenue per package. With the stock down over 50% from 2022 peaks and an expected inflection point in H2 2026, UPS presents a potential turnaround opportunity for long-term investors.

AMZN.title: Down 10% From Its Peak, Is Amazon Stock the Ultimate Summer Buying Opportunity?
AMZN.claim: Amazon stock has declined 10% from its recent all-time highs despite strong Q1 results. The article argues this presents a buying opportunity, highlighting AWS's exceptional 28% revenue growth and $200 billion AI infrastructure investment. Trading at attractive valuations compared to peers, Amazon's multiyear AI growth cycle could deliver substantial returns for long-term investors.

AMZN.title: Is Alphabet the Best "Magnificent Seven" AI Stock?
AMZN.claim: Alphabet's Google Cloud business is growing at 63% year-over-year with operating margins expanding to 32.9%, outpacing rivals like AWS and Azure. The company trades at a market-average 28x earnings despite cloud growth and strong search performance, making it potentially undervalued. However, massive capital expenditure plans ($180-190B in 2026) and regulatory risks pose challenges.

AMZN.title: Berkshire Hathaway's Greg Abel Dumps Amazon and Loads Up on Alphabet. Is It the Better Buy?
AMZN.claim: Berkshire Hathaway's new CEO Greg Abel tripled the company's position in Alphabet while selling Amazon holdings in Q1. The article compares both tech giants, noting Alphabet shows faster growth (22% vs 17%), higher operating margins (36% vs 13.4%), and a lower P/E ratio (27.6 vs 28.4), making it appear the better buy despite Amazon's larger absolute size and strong AWS performance.

AMZN.title: Nvidia, Oracle, and Now SpaceX Are Borrowing Billions Amid the AI Boom. Is This a Warning Sign?
AMZN.claim: Major tech companies are borrowing billions to fund AI infrastructure investments. While highly profitable companies like Nvidia, Amazon, and Alphabet can comfortably cover debt with cash generation, SpaceX's $20 billion bond offering is more concerning given the company's significant losses and negative free cash flow. The borrowing trend warrants monitoring, particularly for cash-burning companies with less margin for error.

AMZN.title: 3 Better Trillion-Dollar Stocks to Buy Instead of SpaceX
AMZN.claim: The article argues that Nvidia, Apple, and Amazon are better investment choices than SpaceX. While SpaceX is valued near $3 trillion based on speculative bets, these three companies have proven business models and stronger fundamentals. Nvidia dominates AI infrastructure with 85% revenue growth, Apple has a resilient ecosystem-based business, and Amazon is on track to reach $1 trillion in revenue by 2028 with established e-commerce and cloud computing leadership.

AMZN.title: Deep Learning Market to Reach $1,963.25 Billion by 2035 as Generative AI and Enterprise Automation Drive Massive AI Investments | Research by SNS Insider
AMZN.claim: The global deep learning market is projected to grow from $125.41 billion in 2025 to $1,963.25 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 31.69%. Growth is driven by generative AI adoption, enterprise automation, and increased AI infrastructure investments across healthcare, finance, automotive, and industrial sectors. The U.S. market is expected to expand from $37.14 billion to $596.02 billion, while Europe grows from $31.98 billion to $451.64 billion.

AMZN.title: Citizen Services AI Market Size to Hit USD 601.18 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
AMZN.claim: The global Citizen Services AI Market is expected to grow from $19.24 billion in 2025 to $601.18 billion by 2035 at a 41.13% CAGR. Growth is driven by digital government transformation mandates, demand for personalized citizen services, and AI-powered solutions for public service delivery. The U.S. market leads globally, projected to reach $256.42 billion by 2035, while Europe is expected to grow to $144.86 billion. Key applications include AI chatbots, fraud detection, and smart governance platforms.

AMZN.title: Amazon's Latest Warehouse Experiment Could Eliminate Millions Of Labor Hours: Report
AMZN.claim: Amazon is piloting a Full Facility Load Balancing (FFLB) system designed to optimize worker movement in its robot-filled warehouses. The system could save millions in labor costs and reduce approximately 7 million labor hours annually by automatically reassigning workers every three minutes based on package volumes. Amazon plans to roll out FFLB across its Amazon Robotic Sortable facilities this year and recently acquired Fauna Robotics, which developed a humanoid robot called Sprout.

AMZN.title: Goldman Sachs Warns Big Tech's $770 Billion AI Spending Frenzy Could Backfire— Here's Why
AMZN.claim: Goldman Sachs warns that major tech companies' massive $770 billion AI capital expenditure in 2026 could significantly harm profitability. The spending is transforming asset-light hyperscalers into capital-intensive enterprises, with depreciation costs expected to rise from 7% to 12% of revenues by 2027. This will compress profit margins and reduce Return on Equity by an average of 700 basis points for the seven largest tech stocks. However, strong Q1 earnings showing AI monetization success provides some optimism for long-term returns.

DHR.title: Global Laboratory Equipment Services Market Size/Share Worth USD 62.83 Billion by 2035 at a 8% CAGR: Healthcare Foresights (Analysis, Outlook, Leaders, Report, Trends, Forecast, Segmentation, Growth Rate, Value, SWOT Analysis)
DHR.claim: The global laboratory equipment services market is projected to grow from USD 28.94 billion in 2025 to USD 62.83 billion by 2035, at a CAGR of 8%. Growth is driven by increased R&D investments in life sciences, stricter regulatory compliance requirements, expansion of biopharma facilities, and adoption of IoT-enhanced predictive maintenance technologies. Repair and maintenance services represent the largest market segment, with North America dominating and Asia Pacific showing the fastest growth.

DHR.title: Danaher Is Down 22% This Year. Is the Masimo Acquisition the Entry Point Investors Have Been Waiting for?
DHR.claim: Danaher's stock has fallen 22% this year following its $9.9 billion acquisition of Masimo, which expands the company into clinical patient monitoring. Despite integration risks and a patent dispute with Apple, Danaher reported strong Q1 results with 3% revenue growth and raised 2026 EPS guidance. The company is seeing 30% year-over-year growth in equipment orders, benefiting from a resurgence in life sciences M&A activity. Trading at 20x forward earnings—below its historical average—the stock may represent a buying opportunity if management successfully integrates Masimo.

North America and Europe are leading markets, with Asia-Pacific emerging as the fastest-growing region.

AMD.title: AMD vs. Marvell Technology: Which Will Be the Next Trillion-Dollar Stock?
AMD.claim: Despite Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's prediction that Marvell Technology will become the next trillion-dollar company, AMD is better positioned to reach that milestone first. AMD's $848B market cap, dominance in the CPU market, and expected 35% CAGR growth in server CPUs through 2030 give it a significant advantage over Marvell's $244B valuation. However, both companies are well-positioned to benefit from the AI boom, particularly as demand for custom ASICs and CPUs increases with the shift toward agentic AI.

AMD.title: 3 Popular Stocks to Steer Clear Of
AMD.claim: The article warns investors to avoid SpaceX, Palantir, and AMD due to overvaluation relative to their financial performance. SpaceX trades at 112 times sales with a $2.1 trillion valuation on $18.7 billion revenue, Palantir at 87 times forward earnings despite 85% revenue growth, and AMD at 73 times forward earnings with only 38% growth. The author recommends Nvidia as a superior alternative in the AI chip space.

AMD.title: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Chip Stock Is Dominating the Inference Era. It Could Be the Biggest Winner of This Megatrend (Hint: It's Not AMD or Broadcom)
AMD.claim: As AI shifts from training to inference workloads, Nvidia dominates the market with 74% share of AI inference chips, significantly outpacing competitors AMD and Broadcom despite their growth in custom AI processors and server CPUs. Nvidia's inference chip revenue of $41 billion in Q1 2026 exceeds combined revenues of its competitors, and the company is expected to maintain its lead with new Vera Rubin processors designed to reduce inference costs.

AMD.title: Nvidia vs. Advanced Micro Devices: What Do Their Revenue Trends Tell Investors?
AMD.claim: Nvidia demonstrates significantly stronger revenue growth and profitability compared to AMD, with Q2 2026 revenue reaching $81.6 billion versus AMD's $10.3 billion in Q1 2026. Nvidia's 72% net income margin far exceeds AMD's 14%, driven by its dominant position in AI data center chips. While AMD shows solid year-over-year growth of 38%, Nvidia's consistent quarter-over-quarter expansion and leadership in the AI semiconductor space position it as the clear market leader.

AMD.title: Application Specific Integrated Circuit Market Size to Grow $38.47 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
AMD.claim: The global Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) market is projected to grow from $20.95 billion in 2025 to $38.47 billion by 2035 at a 6.30% CAGR, driven by surging AI accelerator demand from hyperscalers and rising custom silicon adoption in automotive and industrial sectors. Full custom ASICs are the fastest-growing segment, while consumer electronics and industrial applications lead market demand.

AMD.title: SHP Wealth Management Acquires Shares of 2,059 Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD - MarketBeat
AMD.claim: SHP Wealth Management Acquires Shares of 2,059 Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. $AMD    MarketBeat

AMD.title: Is Marvell Technology Going to $1 Trillion?
AMD.claim: Marvell Technology has tripled year-to-date after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared it would be the next trillion-dollar company. The company posted strong 28% YoY revenue growth in Q1 FY27 with guidance implying 35% YoY growth in Q2, and CEO Matt Murphy expects accelerated growth throughout fiscal 2027 and 2028. New product innovations like the Teralynx T100 networking chip demonstrate competitive advantages, though other AI stocks like AMD and SanDisk may reach the $1 trillion milestone sooner.

AMD.title: 11 Words From Nvidia CFO Colette Kress That Should Have AMD and Intel Investors Worried
AMD.claim: Nvidia unveiled its Vera CPU platform designed for agentic AI workloads, opening a new $200 billion market opportunity. CFO Colette Kress indicated the company has visibility to nearly $20 billion in CPU revenue this year, with major hyperscalers already partnering for deployment. By offering a complete full-stack solution combining CPUs, GPUs, networking, and software, Nvidia is positioned to capture market share from traditional CPU incumbents and increase customer attachment rates.

AMD.title: Advanced Micro Devices vs. Arm Holdings: Which AI CPU Stock Is the Better Buy?
AMD.claim: AMD and Arm Holdings are both positioned to benefit from growing AI CPU demand, particularly in inference and agentic AI workloads. AMD's EPYC CPUs are already seeing strong adoption with 57% data center revenue growth and expectations for 70%+ year-over-year server CPU growth. Arm is expanding beyond licensing into finished chips with its AGI CPU, backed by over $2 billion in customer demand. However, both stocks trade at premium valuations (AMD at 75x and Arm at 179x forward earnings). AMD appears more attractive for near-term revenue visibility, while Arm offers longer-term platform potential with higher execution risk.

TSLA.title: Now That SpaceX Is Public, Should You Ditch Tesla Stock?
TSLA.claim: SpaceX's recent IPO has made it the hot new Elon Musk stock, but the article argues Tesla remains the better investment. While SpaceX has a higher market cap ($2T vs $1.5T), it's losing money ($8.7B annually) and burning cash rapidly ($20B/year) despite $100B+ in cash reserves. Tesla, by contrast, generates nearly $100B in annual revenue, maintains positive free cash flow of $7B yearly, and has been profitable since 2019. The author suggests SpaceX may eventually acquire Tesla given their stated plans for deeper strategic collaboration.

TSLA.title: Right After Becoming a Trillionaire, Elon Musk "Sold" 17.5 Million Tesla Shares. Here's Why This Isn't a Big Deal.
TSLA.claim: Elon Musk exercised 304 million Tesla stock options granted in 2018, using net share settlement to pay the exercise price by withholding 17.5 million shares. Rather than selling, this transaction actually increased Musk's ownership stake to 19.9% and added 286.4 million restricted shares to his position, demonstrating alignment with Tesla shareholders.

The company announced a multi-year deal with OpenAI valued at over $20 billion for 750MW of compute capacity and launched a partnership with Amazon Web Services for fast inference capabilities. Cerebras completed its IPO raising $6.4 billion, the largest semiconductor IPO of all time.

CBRS.title: Cerebras vs. SpaceX: Which Is the Better AI IPO Stock to Buy and Hold for the Next 10 Years?
CBRS.claim: Cerebras and SpaceX are two major AI-linked IPOs with different business models. Cerebras specializes in wafer-scale AI processors for fast inference with strong OpenAI backing ($10B+ deal), while SpaceX leverages Starlink's recurring revenue base and expanding AI infrastructure business. SpaceX is recommended as the stronger 10-year pick due to multiple growth engines and lower dependency on single customers, though Cerebras offers higher upside potential if it executes on its ambitious roadmap.
